what_it_does: This noticeboard block introduces a synthesis of an architectural idea: using an Obsidian vault as a HOST layer underneath the project's fabric while keeping the custom system as the master. It frames the post as a map rather than a decision, mentions that five explorers contributed, and points readers to full explorations in a switcher.

The body is titled 'Obsidian-as-host — synthesis (a map, not a decision)'.
It states five explorers looked at the idea and lists expected synthesis sections: where they converged, gains, lossy aspects, decisive check, and shape of the answer.
It instructs readers to read the five full explorations in the switcher and to comment on any.

It positions the Obsidian vault as a HOST beneath the fabric with the custom system staying master.
It references five full explorations and a switcher, acting as an orientation pointer rather than a self-contained analysis.

contribution: A concise framing label and pointer that an Obsidian-as-host option is under exploration with the custom system remaining master; preserves the 'map not decision' stance and the link to the parent item.

intention_for_embedding: To provide a neutral entry point and orientation for a design discussion about hosting the system on Obsidian while keeping the custom fabric authoritative.
